text:
The last time you got ghosted, how long did you wait before admitting it was over?

If you're like most guys: way longer than the signals indicated.

Take the quiz: learnsignaltheory.com

She's not 'just busy.'

Busy people still make time for what they want.

The signal isn't her schedule. The signal is her priorities.

Find your blind spot: learnsignaltheory.com

Most dating advice tells you what to say.

Signal Theory teaches you what to notice.

Behavior > Narrative.

Quiz: learnsignaltheory.com

There's the story she tells you.

There's the story you tell yourself.

And then there's what's actually happening.

Most guys never learn to tell the difference.

learnsignaltheory.com

"She said she needs space but still texts me every day."

Texts aren't space. Texting is contact.

Read what she does, not what she says she needs.

Quiz: learnsignaltheory.com

Attention isn't attraction.

She can give you both. She can give you one without the other.

Knowing the difference changes everything.

Find out where you are: learnsignaltheory.com

The biggest lie men tell themselves in dating:

"I just need to show her how much I care."

More effort doesn't fix a misread signal. It amplifies the misread.

learnsignaltheory.com

Hot/cold behavior has two explanations:

1. She's genuinely conflicted
2. She's managing your expectations down

Only one of those is worth your time.

Take the quiz: learnsignaltheory.com

She responded immediately for two weeks.

Now it's 6 hours. Then 12. Then 'seen.'

That's not a busy week. That's a withdrawal pattern.

Signal Theory explains why: learnsignaltheory.com

Three types of men when signals go cold:

1. Chase harder (wrong)
2. Go silent (reactive)
3. Read what changed and respond to reality (Signal Theory)

Which are you? learnsignaltheory.com

Every relationship has a signal baseline.

How often she initiates. How fast she responds. How present she is.

When the baseline drops, that's data — not a bad week.

Quiz: learnsignaltheory.com

"But she said she likes me."

People say a lot of things. Behavior doesn't lie the same way words do.

Signal Theory: trust the pattern, not the statement.

learnsignaltheory.com

The reason you keep ending up in the same situation:

You're pattern-matching to hope, not reality.

Signal Theory rewires that.

Take 5 minutes: learnsignaltheory.com

She plans the next date before the current one ends.

She cancels twice and reschedules once.

Those are opposite signals. Know which one you're getting.

Quiz: learnsignaltheory.com

Not every woman who ghosts is a villain.

Some just don't know how to say 'I'm not interested' directly.

But the signal was there. You just weren't reading it.

learnsignaltheory.com

There's a difference between a woman who's nervous and a woman who's uninterested.

Both can look like distance.

One is worth patience. The other isn't.

Take the quiz: learnsignaltheory.com

She introduces you to her friends.
She keeps you separate from her life.

One of these is a green light.
One is a slow exit.

Signal Theory helps you read the difference.

learnsignaltheory.com

Stop asking 'does she like me?'

Start asking 'what is her behavior telling me?'

The first question leads to hope. The second leads to clarity.

Quiz: learnsignaltheory.com

Signal blindness isn't stupidity.

It's what happens when you care more about the outcome than the information.

Every guy has a blind spot. Find yours.

learnsignaltheory.com

What she did on date 1 doesn't matter as much as what happened between date 1 and date 3.

That window tells you everything about trajectory.

Take the quiz: learnsignaltheory.com

7 signals men miss that she's lost interest — a thread 🧵

(Most guys notice #7. Almost nobody catches #2.)

1/ She stops asking questions.

When someone is into you, they want to know you. They probe. They follow up.

When the questions stop, the curiosity has stopped. Pay attention to this one.

2/ Her responses get shorter — but still come fast.

This is the one guys miss. They see the fast reply and think 'good sign.'

But one-word responses to thoughtful messages aren't engagement. They're obligation.

3/ She stops initiating plans — but agrees when you ask.

Agreeing is passive. Initiating is active interest.

If you're always the one reaching out to set something up, notice that. It's a signal about who's driving.

4-7/ Physical distance increases. Future-pacing stops. She 'forgets' details you told her. She's more available for friends than for you.

Any one of these alone = nothing. All four together = a clear pattern.

Know your blind spot: learnsignaltheory.com

The gap between what she's doing and what you're telling yourself — a thread 🧵

This is the one that keeps men stuck for months.

What she's doing: Responding inconsistently, canceling plans, keeping things vague.

What you're telling yourself: 'She's stressed. She's going through something. She just needs more time.'

The gap between those two things is costing you.

The narrative you build fills in the gaps that behavior leaves open.

She doesn't text back for two days → 'She must be overwhelmed.'

Maybe. But 'maybe' isn't a signal. Behavior is a signal. The two days of silence IS the signal.

This isn't about assuming the worst.

It's about reading what's actually there instead of what you want to be there.

Signal Theory doesn't make you cynical. It makes you accurate.

The fix: Stop narrating. Start observing.

Behavior over a consistent window tells you more than any single moment or conversation.

Find out what pattern you're actually in: learnsignaltheory.com
